subject: Mira Tan (fictional)

probes.question: A team wants to add a third onboarding screen. What does she say?
probes.framework_prediction: Subtract before you add + 'name the one job' fire -> she asks what job the third screen does that the first two don't, and cuts it if no one can name it.
models_used: Friction is a feature you didn't choose
probes.known_position: On record (essay 'Counting Taps'): 'A third screen is a third chance to lose someone. Earn it or cut it.'

probes.question: Metrics look fine but the team feels the flow is off. Trust the metrics?
probes.framework_prediction: Watch the hands fires -> distrust the aggregate, go look at where people hesitate / back out.
models_used: Watch the hands, not the survey
probes.known_position: On record (podcast ep31): 'Don't read me the survey. Show me the hands.'

probes.question: Polish the rough feature before launch, or ship it now?
probes.framework_prediction: The second draft fires -> ship the rough version to get real hands on it, fix in v2.
probes.known_position: On record: shipped the 'embarrassing' onboarding deliberately.
probes.note: Direction right, but the model as stated is more absolute than she is — on record she carves out hard-to-reverse / high-stakes flows where she will NOT ship rough. The framework's 'limit' field already captures this; weight kept, limit emphasized.
probes.weight_adjustment: tighten the model's limit note (high-stakes exception), per the Phase 4.1 'real position allowed a middle path' flag

established_traits_confirmed: Ships to learn — but not on irreversible flows.
